intent: An automated Learning Management System designed to bridge the gap between course delivery and performance analysis. The platform allows instructors to build curriculum modules with integrated multimedia support, while students engage through an interactive dashboard. The core value proposition is the automated assessment engine, which utilizes predefined logic trees and regex-based validation for assignments, allowing teachers to provide instant feedback and data-driven learning analytics without manual grading overhead.

entities.name: CurriculumModule
entities.description: A container entity defining learning content, associated multimedia S3 references, and hierarchical metadata.

entities.name: AssessmentLogicTree
entities.description: A node-based structure defining the evaluation flow and criteria for assignments including regex patterns for validation.

entities.name: PerformanceSnapshot
entities.description: An immutable record of a learner's attempt state, containing raw input data, timing metrics, and calculated progress scores.

entities.name: GradingManifest
entities.description: A bridge entity that maps student submissions to specific AssessmentLogicTree rules and tracks execution status of the grading worker.

entities.name: LearningAnalyticsAggregator
entities.description: A materialized view or cache layer that compiles PerformanceSnapshot records into actionable insights for the instructor dashboard.

entities.name: SecurityPrincipal
entities.description: A unified identity model storing OAuth2 scope mappings and role-based access control tokens for users.

entities.name: ExecutionEnvironmentPolicy
entities.description: A configuration entity defining resource limits and isolation parameters for the safe execution of code-based assessment snippets.

architecture.description: The system utilizes a decoupled architecture where the frontend dashboard interacts with a RESTful API gateway secured via OAuth2. When a student initiates an assignment, a PerformanceSnapshot is generated, linking the user to the CurriculumModule. Upon submission, the payload is routed to the GradingManifest, which triggers the asynchronous execution engine. The engine retrieves the relevant AssessmentLogicTree to evaluate the submission against regex patterns or ExecutionEnvironmentPolicy rules. Once processed, the engine updates the PerformanceSnapshot with an automated grade and feedback, which is subsequently ingested by the LearningAnalyticsAggregator. This aggregator computes trends in real-time, providing instructors with high-fidelity performance visualizations through the dashboard, while all heavy media assets remain offloaded to S3.

endpoints.summary: Initiate a new performance snapshot for a learner
Update snapshot with raw data or progress
Trigger grading for a snapshot submission
Retrieve assessment logic and regex rules
Retrieve user identity and scope mappings

rules.description: Ensure immutability of PerformanceSnapshot records once status is set to Finalized to maintain audit integrity.
rules.logic: If PerformanceSnapshot.status == 'Finalized', deny all PATCH and PUT requests to the record.

rules.description: Validate AssessmentLogicTree integrity before assigning to a CurriculumModule to prevent execution failures.
rules.logic: Verify that all regex patterns in AssessmentLogicTree nodes are compilable and that the node structure forms a directed acyclic graph.

rules.description: Enforce ExecutionEnvironmentPolicy constraints during asynchronous code evaluation.
rules.logic: Before spawning the execution container, verify if the resource requirements of the submission payload exceed the limits defined in ExecutionEnvironmentPolicy.

rules.description: Maintain GradingManifest consistency by ensuring every submission status transition is atomic.
rules.logic: The GradingManifest state must transition from 'Pending' to 'Processing' to 'Completed' or 'Failed' strictly in sequence; prevent concurrent transition events.

rules.description: Access Control enforcement for dashboard data based on SecurityPrincipal scopes.
rules.logic: Querying LearningAnalyticsAggregator must validate that the requestor's OAuth2 scope includes 'instructor_view' for the requested ModuleID.

rules.description: Data synchronization validation between PerformanceSnapshot and LearningAnalyticsAggregator.
rules.logic: The LearningAnalyticsAggregator must trigger an update only if the PerformanceSnapshot status is 'Graded' and the calculated progress score is within the range [0, 100].
